title: Protocol for micro-sheet pocket 1: Detailed operation manual of six requirements
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e35cp","text":"This protocol details micro-sheet pocket 1 and detailed operation manual of six requirements.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Examine the average diameter of filter opening by referring to the catalog of the sheet.
Confirm opening size by using scanning electron microscopy (SEM), if possible.
Cut in half the sheet in order to compare each other.
Make-up for water permeability test device
Create a sample reservoir
Cut the attachment part (a) of the tip of the P1000 pipette with scissors from the tip body (b) indicated by red arrow.
For one sheet, cut off at 2 cm from the tip of (b). In the case of two or more sheets, cut off 1.5 cm from the tip. (Red line).
Measure the inner diameter (cm) of the cut side of (b) and calculate the cross-sectional area (inner diameter x inner diameter x 3.14) (Cross-sectional area of test sheet).
Cut a 2 cm area from the opening of the tip of the P200 pipette with scissors (c).
Cut a 3 cm area from the opening of the other P200 pipette tip with scissors (d).
Connect (b) Connect (c) and (d) (yellow arrow).
Cover (b) with the test sheet (3cm×3cm) on the red line.
Fix the sheet by fitting (a) (blue arrow).
Cut the sheet that protrudes from (a).
Seal and fix the connected parts with Parafilm. Complete view of the sample reservoir Assembly diagram of permeability test device
Vertical installation of a water storage tank on the workbench using a Burette stand.
Assemble water storage tank, drainage tank 1, drainage tank 2, according to the reference above diagram.
Adjust the height of the overflow opening to " 5 mL " on the scale of a 50 mL disposable measuring pipette.
Attach the sample reservoir to the end of the reservoir.
Wrap the parafilm to prevent liquid leakage.
Prepare 3 sheets cut in 100 mm x 100 mm sized square.
Measure the mass of a cut sheet in the standard condition to 1 mg -level.
Immerse 3 sheets in the water for at least 00:15:00 .
Remove 3 sheets from the water with tweezers.
Allow the water to drip off for at least 00:01:00
Measure the mass of a cut sheet after wetting and dripping off the water (m2).

Calculate the water retention rate by the following formula m=(m2-m1)/m1 x 100 m: water retention rate (%) m1: mass of the specimen in the standard condition (mg) m2: mass of the specimen after wetting and dripping off the water (mg)
Preparation of sample reservoir Preparation of sample reservoir (consisting of part (a) and (b), we named“filter assembly”) is explained in step 15.1
Observe under a polarized light microscope for sheets with a diameter of filter opening of less than 10 μm .
Examine the thickness by referring to the catalog of the sheet.
After about 00:00:10 , measure the test pieces to 0.1 mm using a vertical ruler.
Lay a glass plate (200 ± 2 mm square, a mass of 82 ± 2 g, a thickness of 0.7 mm ) on the test sheets.
Put 10 test sheets on a horizontal worktable.
Prepare 10 test sheets (cut with scissors to 20 cm x 20 cm square ).
If no obvious change is observed, repeat the procedure 9-12 by changing the test solution (formalin, methanol).
Wash with water, dried, and observe both samples.
Cover the sheet with a lid and kept at Room temperature for about 08:00:00 .
Immerse the whole part of one cut sheet in the test solution (xylene).
Calculate the average value in accordance with JIS A 5209:2014, Rule B (rounding method).
Create a water storage tank Cut off the suction opening of the 50 mL disposable measuring pipette with a plastic cutter.
Create drainage tank 1 Drill a hole to make an overflow port at a height of about 12 cm from the bottom of the container 1 (inner diameter of hole, about 15 mm ) .
Operation Procedure
Fill drainage tank 1 with water until it flows out of the overflow port.
Measure t2-t1 value 5 times.
Repeat the operation in steps 21.1 and 21.2 and confirm that the t2-t1 value was almost constant.
Measure transit time (t2-t1 value) it takes for the water in a water storage tank to pass from the " 40 mL " scale to the " 20 mL " scale using a stopwatch.
Calculation The permeability coefficient was calculated by the following formula, applying JIS A 1218:2020.
Preparation of cell suspension (collection of cells from ascites and/or pleural fluid)
Centrifugation residual specimens (ascites and/or pleural effusion)at 2000 rpm, °C, for 00:05:00 .
Stand still for 00:15:00 .
Add 30 mL to 35 mL of PBS and mix by inverting.
Collect more than 1 mL of cell pellet.
Gently remove the supernatant with an aspirator.
Repeat PBS wash (step 32.4 - 32.6 ).
Gently remove the supernatant with an aspirator.

Counts all cells in a cell suspension Add 90 µL of 0.4% trypan blue solution to 10 µL of cell suspension (10-fold dilution). Gentle vortex for 00:00:02 - 00:00:03 . Inject 6 µL samples with a P20 pipette through the sample inlet of the cell counter plate. Count the number of cells in 4 compartments using the cell counter plate. Calculate the number of cells per 1 µL of stock solution {(average number of cells of 4 compartments) × 10 × (dilution factor)}. Note : The count of one plot should be about 100. If the number of cells is large, dilute the cell suspension 10 to 100 times with PBS. If the number of cells is small, reduce the amount of 0.4% trypan blue solution added to the cell suspension 10 µL .
Adjust to 1 x 10 6 - 10 7 cells/ml in 10% neutral buffered formalin solution.
Operation Procedure
Apply 600 µL of cell suspension (1 x 10 6 -10 7 cells/ml) to the sample reservoir (filter assembly) with a P1000 pipette.

Measure the number of cells in the filtrate Write an enclosure on a glass slide with an alcohol-resistant marking pen. Add 15 µL drops of 95% ethanol in the enclosure, followed promptly by 15 µL drops of filtrate. Allow to natural drying. Perform Papanicolaou staining. Count cells in 10 fields of view at 400x under the microscopy.
Allow to natural filtration.
Fill water storage tank with water by using a funnel.
Set sample reservoir onto 2 mL microtube.
creator: Keiichi Iwaya
